name: Brightlocal
heading: Brightlocal Product Overview
highlight_text: Who it is best for: Small-mid businesses, Agencies
product_overview_description:
Brightlocal Product Overview

Brightlocal is a great local marketing software, with a very appealing UI and some of the best analytics and reporting capabilities in this category. Their listing management solution is manual – a cheaper option for citation submissions but may be costly for updating listings. Their local analytics tools like keyword tracker and Grid ranker offer deep insights into your local performance.

‍

Core Features

  • Focused on high-authority sites (e.g., Manta, Merchant Circle, Tupalo, Hotfrog, Superpages)
  • Dedicated team for creating and optimizing listings on authoritative sites
  • Permanent citation ownership without recurring fees
  • Real-time updates for major platforms (Google, Apple Maps, Facebook, Bing)
  • A streamlined process for establishing and maintaining accurate listings
  • Authority Layer for foundational citations
  • Engagement Layer for high-visibility platforms
  • Combination of expert intervention and API-driven updates
  • Emphasis on authoritative sites for maximum SEO impact

‍

What’s great

✅Stellar insights into competitors and your own local performance

✅Manual submissions ensure you have better control over your listings.

✅Reputation management tool to keep you on top and attract positive feedback from customers

✅Ensures permanent ownership of your listings

✅Highly visual reporting with powerful analytics and insight-capturing capabilities that helps agencies/resellers communicate their results better

‍

What’s not great

❌Manual services may take time and ongoing updates are costlier. It may feel cheaper up front (just $3 to build a citation) but it’s known that your citations need to be updated regularly to keep your NAP consistent. Such updates require you to pay up more to Brightlocal and can easily mean shelling out 30-40% more than most API-based tools like Synup.

❌No additional selling support for agency partners. Brightlocal does offer good reporting and whitelabeling for agencies, but doesn’t have any features to support their business and help them find new clients.

‍

Pricing

Brightlocal offers a 14-day free trial for all of their plans. If billed monthly, the pricing starts at $39 per month for a single location for the base plan, which just includes listing tracking and reporting. For listing management and more advanced features like reputation management, you may pay up to $59 per location per month. Brightlocal also offers a citation submission service priced on a per-citation basis. This doesn’t include managing citations, which may incur extra costs. They call it ‘Simply Listings’, hah, sweet!
